summary: Subscription billing through a contract-stable interface: hosted checkout, a webhook-derived subscription mirror, cancel/reactivate/change-plan, and entitlement — with pluggable, attested payment-vendor adapters.

exports: Billing { createCheckout(input): Promise<CheckoutSession>; getSubscription(userId): Promise<Subscription | null>; cancelAtPeriodEnd(input): Promise<Subscription>; reactivate(input): Promise<Subscription>; changePlan(input): Promise<Subscription>; ingestWebhook(input): Promise<IngestResult>; onSubscriptionChange(handler): Unsubscribe }
class BillingError extends Error { code: BillingErrorCode }

invariants: Importing performs no I/O and never throws; configuration is validated at call time with typed errors and secrets redacted.
Invalid input fails fast with a typed error and zero side effects.
Inbound webhook handling is idempotent under at-least-once delivery (dedupe by vendor event id).
Subscription state derives solely from verified webhook events, never from client input.
Inbound webhooks are verified (timing-safe signature over the raw bytes, with a bounded timestamp window) before any state change.
No card data is ever stored or logged.
The part writes only its own billing_ tables and references the principal by opaque user_id.
Entitlement is exactly status in {active, trialing}.
notes: v1 scope is checkout + subscription lifecycle + entitlement, with the stripe AND paddle adapters attested (paddle added in billing.subscription 1.1.0 — the real, attested vendor-flip). Inbound webhook verification is the part's own (raw-HMAC, per-vendor wire format selected by the adapter's webhookSignatureHeader); composing on webhooks.ingest is a future option. Dunning email (composing on email.transactional) is deferred to a future minor. Further vendor adapters (lemonsqueezy, etc.) are interchangeable by construction against this capability.
